expectations.text: Response presents 2 or more distinct named strategy options before proceeding
expectations.evidence: Three options: 'Option A — Parallel (Expand-Contract)', 'Option B — Branch by Abstraction', 'Option C — Big Bang'. Each is a named, distinct strategy.

expectations.text: Each strategy option includes explicit effort and risk ratings
expectations.evidence: Option A: 'Effort: medium / Risk: low'. Option B: 'Effort: medium-high / Risk: medium'. Option C: 'Effort: low (fastest calendar time...) / Risk: high'. All three include both ratings.

expectations.text: Branch by Abstraction is mentioned as a viable option given the stable public interface
expectations.evidence: Option B is Branch by Abstraction — presented with rationale: 'Keeps callers unchanged longest'. Described as coroutines wrapped behind existing interface via adapter layer. 'Only preferable if keeping the public API stable for an extended period is a hard requirement.'

expectations.text: Agent stops and waits for user to choose a strategy before proceeding to Phase 2
expectations.evidence: 'Which option do you want to proceed with?' — response ends there. No Phase 2 work is started.

expectations.text: Absence of existing unit tests is identified as a factor affecting snapshot cost
expectations.evidence: 'Test coverage: None — no existing unit tests' in the impact table. Also: 'Zero tests means no automated regression detection' (Option C rationale) and Option A/B preparation includes 'Write characterization tests against the *current* RxJava implementation'.

claims.claim: Big Bang is presented as Option C in old_skill instead of the less risky In-place option
claims.evidence: Old skill response includes Big Bang as a named strategy option. With-skill response includes In-place as Option C instead. This is a qualitative difference — old_skill's option set is arguably better calibrated to the scenario (no tests, 4 modules) since it includes Big Bang as a legitimate contrast case.

eval_feedback.overall: Both runs pass all assertions. The two responses are high quality and nearly equivalent in thoroughness. Main difference: with_skill recommends In-place as a cautionary option C; old_skill recommends Big Bang as option C. Both valid choices for a comparison option.
